START.text: Welcome to your end-of-day reflection. Let's review how today went.

Q1_AXIS1.text: When a critical task was blocked today due to external dependencies (e.g., waiting on another team, approval, or system access), what was your immediate next step?

options.label: I created a temporary workaround or immediately pivoted to another high-impact task I could control.
I formally flagged the blocker in our tracking system and monitored the status until it was resolved.

Q2_AXIS1.text: When project requirements or priorities suddenly shifted today, how did you handle the transition?

options.label: I assessed the new constraints and quickly restructured my approach to maintain momentum toward the new goal.
I documented the impact the shift would have on my timeline and requested updated deadlines from leadership.

Q3_AXIS1.text: When faced with an ambiguous request or a brief lacking specific details today, what action did you take?

options.label: I made educated assumptions and built a quick baseline draft to force alignment and keep things moving.
I paused execution and formally requested clarification to ensure I didn't waste time building the wrong thing.

R1_INTERNAL.text: You demonstrated a 'Victor' mindset today. Recognizing your own agency in challenges helps you maintain control and drive outcomes.

R1_EXTERNAL.text: It sounds like you felt the impact of a 'Victim' mindset today, where external factors weighed heavily on you. Remember that while you cannot control everything, you can always control your response.

BRIDGE_1.text: Now, let's move on to how you approached your work and responsibilities.

Q4_AXIS2.text: When you encountered a broken process, missing documentation, or a minor task outside your immediate scope today, how did you respond?

options.label: I took a few extra minutes to document the fix, clean up the artifact, or patch the process for the next person.
I stayed focused strictly on my assigned deliverables to protect my bandwidth and maintain my velocity.

Q5_AXIS2.text: During a team sync, project wrap-up, or milestone today, how did you approach the distribution of credit and visibility?

options.label: I actively looked for an opportunity to elevate a peer's contribution or highlight a collaborative win.
I made sure my specific individual contributions and metrics were clearly documented and visible to leadership.

Q6_AXIS2.text: If you completed your required sprint tickets or daily deliverables before the day was entirely over, what was your next move?

options.label: I reached out to see if any teammates were swamped and offered to offload some of their work.
I used the remaining time to recharge, step away, or focus strictly on my own professional development.

R2_CONTRIB.text: You showed a strong 'Contribution' orientation. By focusing on what you bring to the table rather than what you are owed, you elevate the whole team.

R2_ENTITLED.text: It is natural to expect things from your workplace, but leaning too far into 'Entitlement' can cause frustration. Shifting focus to how you can contribute often brings more fulfillment.

BRIDGE_2.text: Finally, let's reflect on your focus regarding others.

Q7_AXIS3.text: When navigating a difference of opinion, a technical disagreement, or minor friction with a colleague today, what was your primary focus?

options.label: I focused on finding a compromise that kept the team moving forward, even if it meant adjusting my preferred approach.
I made sure my perspective was clearly articulated and backed by data so my rationale was officially understood.

Q8_AXIS3.text: When looking back at the most demanding or stressful moment of your day, what was the primary source of that friction?

options.label: Worrying about how a delay or issue might impact the end-user or cause a bottleneck for my teammates.
Managing my own cognitive load and ensuring I didn't fall behind on my personal KPIs or assigned deliverables.

Q9_AXIS3.text: As you log off and wrap up your day, how are you primarily measuring whether today was a success?

options.label: By looking at what we unblocked together and how much closer the team is to our shared milestone.
By reviewing the specific tickets I closed and ensuring my individual execution and output were flawless.

R3_TEAM.text: You operated with an 'Altrocentric' radius. Your outward focus on the team builds trust and psychological safety for everyone.

R3_SELF.text: Your focus was largely 'Self-Centric' today. While self-care is important, an overly inward focus can sometimes create friction with team goals.

R4_FINAL_INSIGHT_A.text: Incredible job today. You maintained a high level of ownership, contribution, and teamwork across all areas.

R4_FINAL_INSIGHT_B.text: Today had its ups and downs. Reflecting honestly is the first step toward showing up differently tomorrow.

SUMMARY.text: Session Complete. When faced with a blocked task today, you chose to: '{Q1_START.answer}'. When handling undocumented work, your approach was: '{Q4_EXPECTATION.answer}'. Finally, you measured your success today by: '{Q9_SUCCESS.answer}'. Use these insights to shape your mindset for tomorrow.
